commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f8d935dce0e933402fc771ec119f715113c08d26)
commands.review_summary:
Adds optional `gateway.controlUi.timezone`, resolves `host` through the gateway bootstrap payload, and routes Control UI timestamp formatting through a shared timezone helper with config metadata and unit/bootstrap tests.

PR surface: Source +89, Tests +160, Generated 0. Total +249 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Not applicable for the requested feature; the PR describes a Firefox fingerprinting scenario but explicitly says the live Gateway/browser path was not verified. The blocking Usage-label defect is source-reproducible from the changed formatter path and the Node `Intl` date check.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer action is needed to fix the patch defects and add real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's live browser proof.

target.title: feat(gateway): add controlUi.timezone for Control UI timestamp rendering
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Control UI formats all timestamps client-side using browser-local `Date.toLocaleString()` APIs. Browsers with fingerprinting protection (hardened Firefox, Tor-derived configs) report UTC instead of the user's actual timezone. Chat timestamps then render in UTC without any visible indicator, confusing operators who expect local time.
- **Why it matters:** An admin UI should display predictable timestamps. Relying solely on the browser's fingerprinting surface makes timestamps silently wrong for privacy-conscious deployments.
- **What changed:** Added `gateway.controlUi.timezone` config field. Accepts `"host"` (resolves to server local timezone) or any IANA timezone string (e.g. `"America/New_York"`). The resolved timezone travels through the bootstrap config payload to the UI, which passes an explicit `timeZone` option to every `toLocaleString`/`toLocaleTimeString`/`toLocaleDateString` call.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** When `timezone` is omitted, behavior is identical to before this change. No `"browser"` or `"user"` mode enum was added, keeping the config surface minimal. Core gateway logic, auth, and channel code are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #90314

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A (feature request, not a bug fix).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A (new feature).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config key: `gateway.controlUi.timezone` (string, optional).
  - `"host"` resolves to the gateway server's local IANA timezone.
  - Any valid IANA timezone string (e.g. `"Europe/Warsaw"`) is used directly.
  - Omitting the key preserves current browser-default behavior.
- When set, all Control UI timestamp surfaces (chat messages, sessions, logs, usage metrics, dreaming, debug event log) render in the configured timezone.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[browser] -> Date.toLocaleTimeString() -> uses browser-reported TZ (may be UTC under fingerprinting)

After:
[server config] -> bootstrap payload { timezone: "America/New_York" } -> [UI timezone module]
[UI formatter] -> toLocaleTimeString(date, locale, { timeZone: configured }) -> correct local time
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS Darwin 25.5.0
- Runtime: Node 22+, Bun
- Model/provider: N/A (UI-only change)
- Integration/channel: N/A

### Steps

1. Set `gateway.controlUi.timezone` to `"America/New_York"` in config.
2. Open Control UI in a browser with fingerprinting protection enabled (Firefox `privacy.resistFingerprinting = true`).
3. Send a chat message and observe the timestamp in the chat footer.

### Expected

Timestamp renders in America/New_York local time regardless of browser fingerprinting settings.

### Actual

Before this PR: timestamp renders in UTC. After: renders in the configured timezone.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- 12 tests pass: 7 timezone module unit tests (verifying IANA timezone application to all three locale formatters) + 5 bootstrap integration tests (including 2 new tests verifying timezone is set from bootstrap payload and left undefined when omitted).
- `pnpm check` passes (types, lint, import cycles, format).
- `pnpm config:docs:check` passes with regenerated baseline.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified: all 12 timezone-related tests pass, `pnpm check` green, `pnpm tsgo` green, config baseline regenerated and checked.
- Edge cases: omitted timezone preserves browser default, `"host"` resolves server timezone, empty string treated as omitted.
- Not verified: live browser test with `privacy.resistFingerprinting` enabled (no Gateway running in this environment).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes. Omitting the new config key preserves current behavior exactly.
- Config/env changes? Yes, one new optional config key `gateway.controlUi.timezone`.
- Migration needed? No.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Invalid IANA string passed to `toLocaleString` throws a `RangeError` in some runtimes.
  - Mitigation: The Zod schema accepts any string; invalid IANA strings will cause a runtime error on first timestamp render. A follow-up could add Zod refinement validation against `Intl.supportedValuesOf("timeZone")`.
target.author: rishabhkodes
